content.text: The EP-133 features master effects with six effect types plus Punch-In FX 2.0 for live performance. Effects can be applied globally or punched in momentarily during performance.
Press FX/OUTPUT to access master effects. The EP-133 has one global send effect slot. Use +/- to select the effect type, and Y knob to adjust the main parameter.

rows: Tempo-synced echo with feedback and mix control
Modulated doubling for width and movement

content.text: Each sound can have its own effect send amount. In Edit mode (SHIFT + SOUND), press pad 6 (>FX) and use the Y knob to set how much of that sound is sent to the master effect.

content.title: Mix Tip
content.text: Keep drums relatively dry (low FX send) and add more reverb/delay to melodic elements for depth without muddiness.
Punch-In FX are performance effects that apply instantly while holding a pad. OS 2.0 enhanced these effects with pressure sensitivity - press harder for more intense effects.
To use Punch-In FX, hold any number pad during playback. The effect is applied to the entire mix while held, and releases when you let go.

rows: Beat Repeat - Loop a short section of audio
Tape Stop - Pitch drops to silence

content.title: Performance Technique
content.text: Punch-In FX are perfect for drops and transitions. Try Tape Stop (3) before a chorus, or Beat Repeat (1) to build tension.
OS 2.0 introduced sidechain ducking. This automatically reduces the volume of other sounds when a trigger sound (typically kick drum) plays, creating the classic pumping effect.

items: Adjust the ducking amount and release time
Sounds with FX send will now duck when the trigger plays

content.text: Sidechain works well with bass and pad sounds. The kick triggers ducking, making the bass 'pump' in time with the beat.
The audio input can be routed through the master effects for processing external audio. This is useful for adding effects to instruments connected to the EP-133.
